---
title: "Peclet Examples"
subtitle: "See what's possible — then copy, run, and adapt."
toc: false
---

Worked, **runnable** examples for the [`peclet`](https://pypi.org/project/peclet/)
simulation suite: GPU-accelerated particle dynamics, CFD, and the spatial-indexing
primitives they build on. Every example is executed to produce the figures and
numbers you see, ships its source as a downloadable notebook, and links back to
the exact solver API it uses.

New here? Start with the NumPy warm-up, then see the same physics solved by the
real cut-cell immersed-boundary solver.

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Channel flow: the Method of Manufactured Solutions →](examples/channel-mms/index.qmd)
Verify a finite-difference momentum solve against an exact, self-manufactured
profile and measure its order of accuracy — pure NumPy, runs anywhere.

*Methods: finite differences · MMS · grid convergence*
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Poiseuille through an SDF channel (peclet) →](examples/poiseuille-ibm/index.qmd)
The real thing: `peclet.flow`'s Robust-Scaled **cut-cell immersed-boundary**
solver drives body-force channel flow past non-grid-aligned walls and recovers
the analytic parabola to a fraction of a percent.

*Methods: cut-cell IBM · SDF geometry · pressure projection · grid convergence*
:::

:::

::: {.callout-note}
## How these run
Lightweight examples execute in CI and in the browser (Binder/Colab). Solver-backed
examples are executed on hardware with `peclet` installed, and their outputs are
**frozen** into the site so this page always builds — see each example's *Reproduce
this* box.
:::
